calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Administrative Writ
Off Calendar May-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Trevor Montgomery, Esq. of Beckman Blair LLP, (415-871-0070), for Carolyn M. Shaw and William Shaw, Petitioners and moving parties.
Wayne K. Snodgrass, Deputy City Attorney, (415-554-4675), for CCSF, Respondent and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONERs CAROLYN SHAW and WILLIAM SHAW's Motion For Administrative Writ is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden. The Court finds that the Administrative Law Judge and the Rent Board did not abuse its discretion in determining that 2002 Costa-Hawkins Amendment (CC 1954.52(a)(3)(b)(ii)) applies to the conversion at issue and that petitioners are not "bona fide purchasers for value" within the meaning of the amendment. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
